block_00: 🤒 🚑 💊 How to reanimate a losing trade
block_01: First aid for a losing trade
block_04: Sitting on a losing trade? Have no fear, it can still go back to normal if you apply a relevant treatment. You have at least 3 ways to go: close, do nothing, or save the trade with the double down recovery system.
block_05: To decide which treatment to use, answer these questions:
block_06: How big might my loss be and can I afford it?
block_07: Where does the market currently stand?
block_08: Do I have sufficient reserve capital?
block_09: Now, depending on your answers, you can go with one of the three possible treatments.
block_10: Close the trade at a loss
block_11: Indications for use: big position, little or no reserve funds, changing trend
block_12: Sometimes the best idea is to close the position and lose a little — as opposed to losing it all. Before you close the trade, evaluate the current market condition: is it changing or your prediction is playing out as expected? If you can still say “I would buy (sell) now”, the situation may improve.
block_15: Indications for use: medium to big position, stable market, backup money
block_16: If technical and fundamental analysis didn’t reveal any dramatic market changes, the best decision is just to wait. Don’t wait for too long though — that’s dangerous, especially for day traders without long-term commitments.
block_18: Reanimate with double down recovery system
block_19: Indications for use: the position is rather small relative to your account size.
block_20: Doubling down means buying as the market goes against you in order to improve your average order entry price.
block_22: Imagine you bought 100 Tesla shares. Suddenly, the price of Tesla shares dropped. You can save this trade by buying another 100 shares and waiting until they go up again.
block_28: Now you know how to handle losing trades.
block_29: Don’t forget about risk management and don’t be afraid to lose — there’s always a way out!
